name: MONAI (Medical AI Framework)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); NVIDIA + King's College London + others

auth.notes: Local inference; HuggingFace Hub for pretrained models

performance.notes: GPU recommended; 3D volume processing is compute-intensive

agent_readiness.notes: Medical imaging AI framework built on PyTorch. Specialized transforms for medical images (DICOM, NIfTI): Spacingd, Resized, NormalizeIntensityd. Data loaders with medical format support. Models: UNet, ViT, DynUNet, SegResNet. MONAI Bundle system for reproducible experiments. Sliding window inference for large volumes. Auto3DSeg for automated 3D segmentation. MONAI Label for annotation-assisted labeling. Used in FDA-cleared medical AI products. The medical imaging equivalent of HuggingFace Transformers.

gotchas: Regulatory compliance considerations for medical AI
best_when: Medical imaging AI (CT, MRI, X-ray segmentation and classification)
Healthcare AI products needing FDA-cleared toolchain
avoid_when: Teams without medical imaging domain expertise
summary: MONAI is the medical imaging AI framework by NVIDIA and King's College London. PyTorch-based with specialized transforms for DICOM/NIfTI volumes, medical segmentation models (UNet, SegResNet), and sliding window inference. MONAI Bundle for reproducible experiments. Auto3DSeg for automated 3D segmentation. Used in FDA-cleared products. The standard framework for clinical AI development — requires medical imaging domain expertise.
